inline_review:
commit c382388c62c3c472c31fb886dd002ced30ac32a8
Author: Suren Baghdasaryan <surenb@google.com>

mm: use vma_start_write_killable() in process_vma_walk_lock()

This commit replaces vma_start_write() with vma_start_write_killable()
when process_vma_walk_lock() is used with PGWALK_WRLOCK, and adjusts
users to handle the potential -EINTR error.

> --- a/mm/mempolicy.c
> +++ b/mm/mempolicy.c
[ ... ]
> @@ -969,6 +969,7 @@ static const struct mm_walk_ops queue_pages_lock_vma_walk_ops = {
>   *      (a hugetlbfs page or a transparent huge page being counted as 1).
>   * -EIO - a misplaced page found, when MPOL_MF_STRICT specified without MOVEs.
>   * -EFAULT - a hole in the memory range, when MPOL_MF_DISCONTIG_OK unspecified.
> + * -EINTR - walk got terminated due to pending fatal signal.
>   */
>  static long
>  queue_pages_range(struct mm_struct *mm, unsigned long start, unsigned long end,

Does this code accidentally overwrite the new -EINTR return value with
-EFAULT if the walk is interrupted before processing the first VMA?

Looking at the implementation of queue_pages_range():

    err = walk_page_range(mm, start, end, ops, &qp);

    if (!qp.first)
        /* whole range in hole */
        err = -EFAULT;

If walk_page_range() returns -EINTR on the first VMA, qp.first will remain
NULL and err will be overwritten with -EFAULT instead of propagating -EINTR.
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"queue_pages_range() incorrectly overwrites -EINTR with -EFAULT if walk_page_range() is interrupted before processing the first VMA.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"The patch modifies `process_vma_walk_lock()` to use `vma_start_write_killable()`, allowing it to return `-EINTR` if a fatal signal is pending. `queue_pages_range()` was updated to document this new `-EINTR` return value. However, if `walk_page_range()` aborts on the very first VMA due to a pending signal, the `queue_pages_test_walk()` callback is never executed, leaving the `qp.first` pointer as `NULL`. Following the walk, the function unconditionally executes `if (!qp.first) err = -EFAULT;`, which overwrites the `-EINTR` error code with `-EFAULT`. This masks the interrupted state and propagates an incorrect error code (`EFAULT` instead of `EINTR`) back to system calls like `mbind()`. Because this only occurs when a fatal signal is pending (meaning the process will terminate shortly), the practical impact is mostly limited to incorrect error reporting and tracing, making it a Medium severity logic error."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit c382388c62c3c472c31fb886dd002ced30ac32a8
Author: Suren Baghdasaryan <surenb@google.com>

mm: use vma_start_write_killable() in process_vma_walk_lock()

This commit replaces vma_start_write() with vma_start_write_killable()
when process_vma_walk_lock() is used with PGWALK_WRLOCK, and adjusts
users to handle the potential -EINTR error.

> --- a/mm/mempolicy.c
> +++ b/mm/mempolicy.c
[ ... ]
> @@ -969,6 +969,7 @@ static const struct mm_walk_ops queue_pages_lock_vma_walk_ops = {
>   *      (a hugetlbfs page or a transparent huge page being counted as 1).
>   * -EIO - a misplaced page found, when MPOL_MF_STRICT specified without MOVEs.
>   * -EFAULT - a hole in the memory range, when MPOL_MF_DISCONTIG_OK unspecified.
> + * -EINTR - walk got terminated due to pending fatal signal.
>   */
>  static long
>  queue_pages_range(struct mm_struct *mm, unsigned long start, unsigned long end,

Does this code accidentally overwrite the new -EINTR return value with
-EFAULT if the walk is interrupted before processing the first VMA?

Looking at the implementation of queue_pages_range():

    err = walk_page_range(mm, start, end, ops, &qp);

    if (!qp.first)
        /* whole range in hole */
        err = -EFAULT;

If walk_page_range() returns -EINTR on the first VMA, qp.first will remain
NULL and err will be overwritten with -EFAULT instead of propagating -EINTR.

"}
